title: Search a 2D Matrix
problem_statement: Write an efficient algorithm that searches for a target value in an m x n matrix with each row sorted and rows connected end-to-start.
constraints: Each row is sorted and the first element of each row is greater than the last element of the previous row.
intuition: Treat the matrix as a flattened sorted array and use binary search on a virtual index.

approaches.name: Virtual Flattened Binary Search
approaches.description: Map a 1D index to 2D coordinates and binary search across the whole matrix.

interview_qa.q: How do you map the 1D index to the matrix?
interview_qa.a: Row = index / n and column = index % n if n is the number of columns.

interview_qa.q: Why is the matrix treated as sorted?
interview_qa.a: The row-wise ordering and the condition between rows make the flattened array globally sorted.
